behavior_summary: Manages an 'earth egg' object's trigger-area lifecycle and post-hatch behavior.
When an actor leaves the egg's fast-area trigger, the script checks the egg's state id and, if the id is 7, rolls it back to 6 and clears the area's reference to the egg.

A separate 'hatch' handler advances egg states, performs gated actions against multiple NPCs (only acting on NPCs that are alive), spawns utility/timed tasks between work blocks, and may spawn a persistent monitor coroutine to continue repetitive post-hatch behavior.
The persistent monitor coroutine periodically re-checks NPCs and re-issues the same spawn/actions over time until stopped.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: U8P_EARTHEGG::leaveFastArea runs on a U8P_EARTHEGG::leaveFastArea trigger and performs a short, deterministic cleanup sequence.
State-branching: U8P_EARTHEGG::hatch heavily branches on Egg::getEggId() to decide which actions to take and which NPCs to target.
Gated spawning: Before each spawn of METHOD::15E7 the code calls Npc::isDead(npcId) and only issues the spawn if that NPC is alive.
Cooperative background work: U8P_EARTHEGG::hatch uses suspend points and spawns U8P_EARTHEGG::04F8 to create ongoing, time-sliced monitoring/interaction behavior.
open_questions: What are the semantic meanings of the egg id values (e.g., 6 vs 7) beyond the observed transitions? (inferred only)
What exact gameplay effect METHOD::15E7 produces when spawned (damage, animation, attach, AI command)?
What does FREE::1C19 do semantically—purely timing/cleanup or other state changes?
Which concrete NPC ids are checked and what are their in-game roles (the disassembly references ids but not named NPCs)?
What conditions stop/terminate the persistent U8P_EARTHEGG::04F8 coroutine?

main_function.name: U8P_EARTHEGG::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: Handle actor exit from the egg's fast-area trigger: check and adjust the egg's state id (change 7 to 6 if observed) and clear the area's reference/trigger association for the egg.
entry_conditions: Event: U8P_EARTHEGG::leaveFastArea — invoked when the Avatar or another actor leaves the egg's fast-area trigger region.

function_map.name: U8P_EARTHEGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Area-exit cleanup and state rollback for the egg object.

function_map.name: U8P_EARTHEGG::hatch
function_map.role: Handle the hatch event: evaluate egg id state, perform NPC-gated spawns, advance egg id(s), and spawn the persistent monitor coroutine.

function_map.name: U8P_EARTHEGG::04F8
function_map.role: Background monitor coroutine: repeatedly re-check NPCs and re-issue spawns/utility tasks on a loop with suspend points.

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: Read the egg object's current state identifier.

function_map.name: Egg::setEggId
function_map.role: Set or update the egg object's state identifier.

function_map.name: U8P_EARTHEGG::resetRef
function_map.role: Clear/reset a reference id association for the given object (used to detach the egg from the trigger/ref).

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Query whether a specified NPC is dead; used as a gating condition before issuing spawns.

function_map.name: spawn METHOD::15E7
function_map.role: Spawn a method/action against a target (used repeatedly for NPC interactions/effects).

function_map.name: spawn FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawn a timed/utility/cleanup action used between work blocks.

function_map.name: spawn U8P_EARTHEGG::04F8
function_map.role: Spawn the persistent monitor coroutine for the egg (internal helper spawned via engine spawn).

internal_calls.purpose: Start a background monitor coroutine to continue repeated post-hatch behavior asynchronously.

external_calls.purpose: Read the egg's current state id to decide whether to change state on exit.
If egg id == 7, set it to 6 to roll back the hatch/progress state on area exit.
Clear the reference/trigger association (ref id 0x0205) between the area and the egg object as part of cleanup.
Drive branching logic to determine which sets of NPC checks/spawns to perform based on egg stage.
Gate spawn actions so they only run for NPCs that are still alive.
Trigger per-NPC actions/interactions when that NPC is alive (exact effect inferred).
Insert a timed/utility action between blocks of hatch work (likely pacing or cleanup).
Repeatedly check NPC death state as part of ongoing monitoring.
Re-issue per-NPC actions from the monitor loop for NPCs that remain alive.
Periodically spawn utility/timed tasks inside the monitor loop.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Manages an 'earth egg' object's trigger-area lifecycle and post-hatch behavior.
